annotations.label: Planar graph

anchor.snippet: a planar graph is a graph that can be embedded in the plane

annotations.note: Mathlib's SimpleGraph library contains no notion of planar graph or planar embedding.

annotations.label: Plane graph / planar embedding

anchor.snippet: Such a drawing is called a plane graph

annotations.note: No formalization of plane graph / drawing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plane graph (formal)

anchor.snippet: A plane graph can be defined as a planar graph with a mapping from every node to a point on a plane

annotations.note: No formalization of a vertex-to-plane mapping with non-crossing edges in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plane–sphere equivalence via stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: Every graph that can be drawn on a plane can be drawn on the sphere as well

annotations.note: Although Mathlib has stereographic projection (Geometry/Manifold/Instances/Sphere), the graph-theoretic equivalence is absent.

annotations.label: Planar map

anchor.snippet: An equivalence class of topologically equivalent drawings on the sphere

annotations.note: Equivalence classes of graph drawings on the sphere are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kuratowski's theorem

anchor.snippet: A finite graph is planar if and only if it does not contain a subgraph that is a subdivision of the complete graph

annotations.note: No planarity or graph-subdivision API in Mathlib; the only 'Kuratowski' file is the metric-space embedding.

annotations.label: Subdivision of a graph

anchor.snippet: A subdivision of a graph results from inserting vertices into edges

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of graph subdivision in SimpleGraph.

annotations.label: Wagner's theorem

anchor.snippet: A finite graph is planar if and only if it does not have

annotations.note: Wagner's theorem is absent from Mathlib, as is the necessary graph-minor infrastructure.

annotations.label: Minor of a graph

anchor.snippet: A minor of a graph results from taking a subgraph and repeatedly contracting an edge into a vertex

annotations.note: No edge-contraction or minor relation defined for SimpleGraph in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robertson–Seymour theorem

anchor.snippet: Klaus Wagner asked more generally whether any minor-closed class of graphs is determined by a finite set

annotations.note: Robertson–Seymour and minor-closed-class theory are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear-time planarity testing

anchor.snippet: for a graph with n vertices, it is possible to determine in time

annotations.note: Mathlib has no planarity algorithm or complexity statement.

annotations.label: Edge bound for planar graphs (Theorem 1)

anchor.snippet: For a simple, connected, planar graph with v vertices and e edges and f faces

annotations.note: No edge-count bound for planar graphs is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: K_{3,3} is not planar (via Theorem 2)

anchor.snippet: The graph K 3,3 , for example, has 6 vertices, 9 edges, and no cycles of length 3

annotations.note: Non-planarity of K_{3,3} is not stated in Mathlib (no planarity predicate exists).

annotations.label: Whitney's planarity criterion

anchor.snippet: Whitney's planarity criterion gives a characterization based on the existence of an algebraic dual

annotations.note: No matroid/algebraic-dual planarity characterization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mac Lane's planarity criterion

anchor.snippet: Mac Lane's planarity criterion gives an algebraic characterization of finite planar graphs

annotations.note: Mac Lane's cycle-space criterion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fraysseix–Rosenstiehl planarity criterion

anchor.snippet: The Fraysseix–Rosenstiehl planarity criterion gives a characterization based on the existence of a bipartition

annotations.note: Fraysseix–Rosenstiehl criterion is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schnyder's theorem

anchor.snippet: Schnyder's theorem gives a characterization of planarity in terms of partial order dimension

annotations.note: Schnyder's theorem on incidence-poset dimension is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Colin de Verdière's planarity criterion

anchor.snippet: Colin de Verdière's planarity criterion gives a characterization based on the maximum multiplicity

annotations.note: The Colin de Verdière invariant is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hanani–Tutte theorem

anchor.snippet: The Hanani–Tutte theorem states that a graph is planar if and only if it has a drawing in which each independent pair of edges crosses an even number of times

annotations.note: Hanani–Tutte is absent from Mathlib (no crossing-parity / drawing infrastructure).

annotations.label: Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: Euler's formula states that if a finite, connected , planar graph is drawn in the plane without any edge intersections

annotations.note: V − E + F = 2 for planar graphs is not in Mathlib (Mathlib's 'Euler formula' files are about number theory/analysis).

annotations.label: Butterfly graph satisfies Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: As an illustration, in the butterfly graph given above

annotations.note: No butterfly-graph example or planar Euler computation in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sparseness from Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: In a finite, connected , simple , planar graph, any face (except possibly the outer one) is bounded by at least three edges

annotations.note: Face-degree / e ≤ 3v−6 style consequences are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Steinitz's theorem

anchor.snippet: Steinitz's theorem says that the polyhedral graphs formed from convex polyhedra are precisely the finite 3-connected simple planar graphs

annotations.note: Steinitz's theorem characterizing polyhedral graphs is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Average degree of planar graphs is less than 6

anchor.snippet: Connected planar graphs with more than one edge obey the inequality

annotations.note: No planarity predicate, hence no average-degree bound, in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kissing/osculating circles

anchor.snippet: We say that two circles drawn in a plane kiss (or osculate ) whenever they intersect in exactly one point

annotations.note: Mathlib has circles in Euclidean geometry but no notion of tangent/kissing circles for coin graphs.

annotations.label: Coin graph

anchor.snippet: A "coin graph" is a graph formed by a set of circles, no two of which have overlapping interiors

annotations.note: Coin graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circle packing theorem (Koebe)

anchor.snippet: The circle packing theorem , first proved by Paul Koebe in 1936, states that a graph is planar if and only if it is a coin graph

annotations.note: The Koebe circle packing theorem is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fáry's theorem (via coin graphs)

anchor.snippet: This result provides an easy proof of Fáry's theorem , that every simple planar graph can be embedded in the plane in such a way that its edges are straight line segments

annotations.note: Fáry's theorem on straight-line embeddings is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Meshedness coefficient / density

anchor.snippet: The meshedness coefficient or density D of a planar graph, or network, is the ratio of the number

annotations.note: Meshedness coefficient is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounds on density D

anchor.snippet: The density obeys

annotations.note: No density bounds for planar graphs in Mathlib (since density is undefined).

annotations.label: Dual graph G*

anchor.snippet: Given an embedding G of a (not necessarily simple) connected graph in the plane without edge intersections, we construct the dual graph

annotations.note: Mathlib has no planar-dual graph construction.

annotations.label: Double dual G** = G

anchor.snippet: The term "dual" is justified by the fact that

annotations.note: No planar dual exists in Mathlib, so no double-dual result either.

annotations.label: Maximal planar graph

anchor.snippet: A simple graph is called maximal planar if it is planar but adding any edge

annotations.note: Maximal planar graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal planar implies 3-connected

anchor.snippet: Every maximal planar graph on more than 3 vertices is at least 3-connected

annotations.note: No k-connectivity ↔ maximal planarity statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Edge and face count of maximal planar graphs

anchor.snippet: If a maximal planar graph has v vertices with

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks counts e = 3v−6, f = 2v−4 for triangulations.

annotations.label: Apollonian networks

anchor.snippet: Apollonian networks are the maximal planar graphs formed by repeatedly splitting triangular faces

annotations.note: Apollonian networks are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strangulated graphs

anchor.snippet: Strangulated graphs are the graphs in which every peripheral cycle is a triangle

annotations.note: Strangulated graphs and peripheral cycles are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Outerplanar graph

anchor.snippet: Outerplanar graphs are graphs with an embedding in the plane such that all vertices belong to the unbounded face

annotations.note: Outerplanar graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: K_4 is planar but not outerplanar

anchor.snippet: Every outerplanar graph is planar, but the converse is not true

annotations.note: No outerplanarity in Mathlib, so the K_4 distinction is absent.

annotations.label: Outerplanar Kuratowski-style characterization

anchor.snippet: A theorem similar to Kuratowski's states that a finite graph is outerplanar if and only if it does not contain a subdivision

annotations.note: No outerplanarity characterization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-outerplanar embedding

anchor.snippet: A 1-outerplanar embedding of a graph is the same as an outerplanar embedding

annotations.note: k-outerplanar embeddings are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Halin graph

anchor.snippet: A Halin graph is a graph formed from an undirected plane tree (with no degree-two nodes) by connecting its leaves into a cycle

annotations.note: Halin graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every Halin graph is planar

anchor.snippet: Every Halin graph is planar

annotations.note: Neither Halin graphs nor planarity are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Upward planar graph

anchor.snippet: An upward planar graph is a directed acyclic graph that can be drawn in the plane with its edges as non-crossing curves

annotations.note: Upward planar graphs are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-completeness of upward planarity testing

anchor.snippet: Not every planar directed acyclic graph is upward planar, and it is NP-complete to test

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize complexity-theoretic results like NP-completeness of upward planarity.

annotations.label: Convex planar graph

anchor.snippet: A planar graph is said to be convex if all of its faces (including the outer face) are convex polygons

annotations.note: Convex planar embeddings are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: K_{2,4} has no convex embedding

anchor.snippet: Not all planar graphs have a convex embedding

annotations.note: No convex-embedding framework, so no K_{2,4} counterexample, in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tutte's spring theorem

anchor.snippet: Tutte's spring theorem even states that for simple 3-vertex-connected planar graphs the position of the inner vertices can be chosen to be the average of its neighbors

annotations.note: Tutte's spring/barycentric embedding theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Word-representable planar graphs include 3-colourable planar graphs

anchor.snippet: Word-representable planar graphs include triangle-free planar graphs

annotations.note: Word-representable graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic count of labeled planar graphs

anchor.snippet: The asymptotic for the number of (labeled) planar graphs on

annotations.note: No asymptotic enumeration of planar graphs in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Almost all planar graphs have exponentially many automorphisms

anchor.snippet: Almost all planar graphs have an exponential number of automorphisms

annotations.note: Automorphism-count asymptotics for planar graphs are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounds on unlabeled planar graphs

anchor.snippet: The number of unlabeled (non-isomorphic) planar graphs on

annotations.note: Counts of unlabeled planar graphs are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four color theorem

anchor.snippet: The four color theorem states that every planar graph is 4- colorable

annotations.note: Mathlib has SimpleGraph.Colorable but no Four Color Theorem (and no planarity).

annotations.label: Fáry's theorem and universal point sets

anchor.snippet: Fáry's theorem states that every simple planar graph admits a representation as a planar straight-line graph

annotations.note: Fáry's theorem and universal point sets are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scheinerman's conjecture

anchor.snippet: Scheinerman's conjecture (now a theorem) states that every planar graph can be represented as an intersection graph of line segments

annotations.note: Scheinerman's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Planar separator theorem

anchor.snippet: The planar separator theorem states that every

annotations.note: The Lipton–Tarjan planar separator theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Planar product structure theorem

anchor.snippet: The planar product structure theorem states that every planar graph is a subgraph of the strong graph product

annotations.note: The planar product structure theorem (Dujmović et al.) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear-time planar graph isomorphism

anchor.snippet: For two planar graphs with v vertices, it is possible to determine in time

annotations.note: Mathlib has no planar-graph isomorphism algorithm.

annotations.label: Bound on maximal cliques in planar graphs

anchor.snippet: Any planar graph on n nodes has at most

annotations.note: While SimpleGraph has cliques, no bound specific to planar graphs exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tutte's theorem on Hamiltonian cycles

anchor.snippet: According to Tutte's theorem on Hamiltonian cycles , every 4-vertex-connected planar graph has a Hamiltonian cycle

annotations.note: Mathlib has SimpleGraph.Hamiltonian but no planarity-based Tutte Hamiltonicity theorem.

annotations.label: Apex graph and k-apex graph

anchor.snippet: An apex graph is a graph that may be made planar by the removal of one vertex

annotations.note: Apex graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1-planar and k-planar graph

anchor.snippet: A 1-planar graph is a graph that may be drawn in the plane with at most one simple crossing per edge

annotations.note: k-planar graphs are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Map graph

anchor.snippet: A map graph is a graph formed from a set of finitely many simply-connected interior-disjoint regions in the plane

annotations.note: Map graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Toroidal graph and graph genus

anchor.snippet: A toroidal graph is a graph that can be embedded without crossings on the torus

annotations.note: Toroidal graphs and graph genus are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Any graph embeds crossing-free in 3-space

anchor.snippet: Any graph may be embedded into three-dimensional space without crossings

annotations.note: Topological graph embeddings into R^3 are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linklessly embeddable graphs

anchor.snippet: a three-dimensional analogue of the planar graphs is provided by the linklessly embeddable graphs

annotations.note: Linklessly embeddable graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Petersen-family characterization of linklessly embeddable graphs

anchor.snippet: the linklessly embeddable graphs may be characterized as the graphs that do not contain as a minor any of the seven graphs in the Petersen family

annotations.note: The Petersen-family/Robertson–Seymour–Thomas theorem is not in Mathlib.
